data.title: NVDA, PLTR: Daniel Ives Hails Nvidia and Palantir as AI Powerhouses at Davos
data.description:
Wedbush's top analyst, Daniel Ives, shared insights from the World Economic Forum in Davos, where he met with global tech executives. He noted that the AI revolution remained the key theme, despite the ongoing geopolitical tensions involving Greenland. Ives was particularly impressed that Nvidia (NVDA) and Palantir Technologies (PLTR) stood out as prime beneficiaries of what he calls the "fourth industrial revolution."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ives emphasized that talks centered on AI investment, adoption, and monetization. He asserted, "The one thing that is clear from Davos is the U.S. tech world is dominating the AI Revolution with China a distant second."

Ives is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #390 out of 12,040 analysts tracked. He has a 56% success rate and an average return per rating of 16.80%.

Nvidia Is the Central Force Behind AI

Ives noted that Nvidia remains the central force powering global AI development. He expects soaring demand from AI hyperscalers such as Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Alphabet (GOOGL) to fuel the next growth phase, as Nvidia's GPUs (graphics processing units) power their AI tech and software.

Despite investor worries about high valuations and the massive capital investments required to sustain AI, Ives believes the market is understating the opportunity. He projects about $3 trillion in AI-related outlays from enterprises and governments over the next three years, with more than $550 billion already underway.

Wedbush has a Buy rating on NVDA and a $230 price target, implying 25.5% upside potential.

Palantir Is a Clear Star in the AI Revolution

Ives stated that Palantir stands out as one of the largest beneficiaries of the AI revolution. As companies shift from AI hype to real-world results, Palantir's Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) helps translate investments into measurable revenue.

Ives positions Palantir as a frontrunner in the AI monetization era, stemming from its ability to deliver actionable AI solutions that align with enterprise needs. Its actionable solutions set it apart, enabling chief information officers (CIOs) across sectors to turn strategic plans into business gains.

Ives has a Buy rating and a $230 price target on PLTR, suggesting 39.1% upside potential.

Big Tech Earnings to Validate the Boom

Ives anticipates the Q4 earnings season will validate the AI investment boom, led by cloud and AI giants. He noted that Wedbush's field research shows strong enterprise AI demand for Microsoft, Alphabet, and Amazon during the quarter. Ives expects capex plans will ramp up even more in 2026.

Ending Thoughts

Ives compared the current moment to mid-1996 rather than the overheated 1999 bubble, noting that AI-driven growth remains in its infancy and is yet to be priced into tech valuations. Looking ahead, Ives predicts that expanding real-world AI applications in software and semiconductors will dominate the next 12 to 18 months, bolstering Wedbush's bullish outlook as the AI revolution accelerates.

Using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool we observe that NVDA stock has a "Strong Buy" consensus and a 43.7% upside potential over the next twelve months, signaling solid analyst conviction in the stock. Meanwhile, Wall Street remains sidelined on PLTR after its 134% stock price surge over the past year.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Cathie Wood Buys Netflix and AI Stocks, Dumps Beam, PINS, and GitLab Shares
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) disclosed their daily ETF trades for Wednesday, January 21, 2026. The firm added to streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) and several AI stocks, while trimming exposure to select biotech and software stocks.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Which Stocks Did Wood Buy on January 21?

1. Netflix Stock – Wood made her largest buy of the day in Netflix, purchasing 83,368 shares through the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) for about $7.27 million, signaling confidence in Netflix's long-term growth. Netflix shares fell 2% yesterday even after reporting better-than-expected Q4 results as investors remain more focused on Netflix's proposed all-cash deal for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) than the quarter itself.  Netflix posted fourth-quarter revenue of $12.1 billion, modestly above Wall Street expectations of $11.97 billion, while adjusted earnings came in at $0.56 per share, also slightly ahead of forecasts of $0.55 per share.

    2. Tempus AI Stock (TEM) – Wood bought 89,501 shares through the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) and the ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG), investing about $5.98 million to expand her AI-driven healthcare exposure.  TEM shares dipped 2.2% on January 21. The move comes as AI tools see wider use in health care data work.

    3. WeRide Stock (WRD) – Wood bought 111,439 shares of WeRide through the ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ), worth about $945,000. The buy follows earlier purchases as self-driving tests and pilot programs continue to expand. WRD shares rose 4.3% on January 21.

    4. Trimble (TRMB) – Wood bought 8,817 shares of Trimble through the ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX) for about $614,000. The purchase comes as demand remains steady for industrial software and positioning tools used in construction and aerospace projects.

    To compare how these newly added positions stack up, let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Intel Ready to Break Higher? Options Traders Jump In as Analysts Weigh Earnings
data.description:
Intel (INTC) surged on Wednesday, jumping nearly 12% and moving close to a new 52-week high to close at $54.25. The rally comes just one day before the chipmaker is set to report its Q4 earnings, putting the stock back in focus. Wall Street analysts expect Intel to report earnings per share of $0.08 on revenues of $13.40 billion.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

With the earnings report due after the close on Thursday, many investors now see the results as a key trigger for Intel's next move.

Options Activity Signals Rising Retail Interest

One clear sign of growing interest showed up in the options market. By shortly after 11 a.m. on Wednesday, call option trading had already reached levels close to Intel's full-day average over the past 10 sessions.

Call options are often used by retail traders to bet on short-term gains. The sharp pickup in activity suggests many traders are positioning for a strong move following Q4 earnings.

Online chatter supports that view. Around midday, Intel ranked among the top five most mentioned stocks on Reddit's WallStreetBets forum over the past 24 hours, pointing to rising attention from individual investors.

According to TipRanks' Options tool, options traders are expecting about a 9.31% move in either direction in Intel stock in reaction to Q4 results.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Rise as Global Tensions Ease Ahead of Inflation Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures rose late Wednesday as global tensions eased, helping markets rebound after a sharp selloff earlier in the week. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.46%, 0.18%, and 0.31%, respectively, at 8:41 p.m. EDT on January 21.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Earlier in the session, stocks jumped sharply after President Donald Trump said he would not move forward with new tariffs on Europe that were set to begin on Feb. 1. He also announced a "framework" deal related to Greenland, which helped calm investor concerns.

Looking ahead, investors are now turning their attention to Thursday morning. Markets are waiting for the release of the personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index, a key inflation measure.

In addition, earnings remain in focus. Procter & Gamble (PG), Intel (INTC), and GE Aerospace (GE) are scheduled to report quarterly results on Thursday, which could further influence market sentiment.

data.title: Intel Stock (INTC) Scores an Upgrade Ahead of Earnings on Server CPU Strength
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) is set to report its 4Q25 results on Thursday, with the earnings report shaping up as a key test of the company's turnaround narrative. So far, the market is buying into the story, with shares up 116% over the past year and 33% just this month.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

HSBC analyst Frank Lee has also become more constructive on Intel's prospects, upgrading his rating from Reduce (i.e., Sell) to Hold (i.e., Neutral). At the same time, Lee raised his price target from $26 to $50. (To watch Lee's track record, click here)

Given uncertainty around its customer pipeline and execution challenges within the foundry segment, along with limited visibility into 'growth drivers' in the core business, Lee had been cautious on Intel. That said, his outlook has improved, as he now expects traditional server demand to return to growth. Boosted by "rising agentic AI," Lee sees "overwhelmingly increasing demand" for server CPUs and forecasts FY26 server shipments will rise 15%–20% year-over-year, compared with Street expectations of 4%–6%.

The growth, says the analyst, is still being underestimated by the Street. As AI evolves from generative models toward agentic AI, Lee sees this transition as a "significant catalyst" for increased server CPU demand.

As for the upcoming print, Lee doesn't anticipate any big surprises in the Q4 results. The analyst is calling for revenue of $13.3 billion, which is generally in line with the consensus estimate of $13.4 billion and within management's guidance range of $12.8 billion to $13.8 billion. Lee expects gross margins to hit 36.5%, in the ballpark of both the Street and management's guidance at 36.3% and 36.5%, respectively.

The Lunar Lake processor has been a drag on gross margins due to the integration of memory directly into the CPU package, with Intel sourcing memory from third parties and relying on external foundries, resulting in memory being passed through to customers at cost. However, given the sharp rise in DRAM prices, Lee sees potential upside to Lunar Lake's margins if Intel has been able to increase ASPs for the product.

While no big surprises are expected in the readout, Lee thinks there could be an upside surprise in the Q1 guide. For 1Q26, Lee is forecasting revenue of $13.2 billion, ahead of the consensus estimate of $12.5 billion, and the analyst anticipates DCAI revenue will begin contributing meaningfully to growth from Q1 onwards. Lee estimates a gross margin of 37%, above the Street's forecast of 36.1%, driven by a more favorable product mix.

Lee now joins 18 other analysts on the fence, while an additional 9 Buys and 4 Sells still make the consensus view a Hold (i.e., Neutral). Meanwhile, the $44.55 average price target sits ~18% below the current share price. (See INTC stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Why AMD Stock Is Up Today — and What a 5-Star Analyst Sees Next
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock rose Wednesday after the chipmaker announced changes to its Board of Directors. The company appointed KC McClure to its board, adding deep finance experience. Meanwhile, in a note today, top KeyBanc analyst John Vinh reiterated a Buy rating on AMD and kept his $270 price target. The five-star analyst expects stronger fourth-quarter results and higher guidance, driven by solid server demand and growing momentum in AI.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Top Analyst Is Upbeat About AMD Stock

Vinh said demand for AMD's server CPUs remains very strong, helped by its latest-generation Turin chips. According to the analyst, large cloud customers are already locking in capacity for 2026, and AMD is close to being sold out of server CPUs through the end of that year.

Because of this tight supply, Vinh said AMD is considering price increases of around 10% to 15%, which could further support revenue and margins.

On the AI side, Vinh said revenue visibility is improving. He expects AMD's AI GPU revenue to reach $14 billion to $15 billion in 2026, supported by rising shipments of its MI300-series successors.

Looking ahead, Vinh expects better results and stronger guidance. He forecasts fourth-quarter revenue of $9.75 billion and EPS of $1.36. Both are above Wall Street estimates. For the first quarter of 2026, he expects revenue of $9.75 billion and EPS of $1.35.

What Investors Are Watching as Q4 Approaches

Vinh said sentiment on AMD is mixed. Bulls highlight strong server CPU demand and steady progress in AI, while bears worry about GPU volumes and tough competition from Nvidia (NVDA).

Looking ahead, he said investors will be focused on three things:

  1. the production timeline and customer commitments for AMD's MI455 and Helios systems
  2. updates on AI revenue growth in 2025 and 2026, and
  3. supply and demand trends in the traditional server market.

Is AMD Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for AMD is Strong Buy, based on 26 Buy and eight Hold ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average AMD stock price target of $284.29, representing a potential 22.58% upside for the shares.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Amazon (AMZN) Launches New AI Healthcare Assistant
data.description:
E-commerce giant Amazon (AMZN) has launched a new artificial intelligence (AI) healthcare assistant for members of its primary care chain called One Medical.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Amazon joins competitors OpenAI and Anthropic, each of which recently launched similar AI healthcare assistants. Amazon's new tool, called Health AI, uses large language models to answer people's health questions and provide them with advice based on their medical records, lab results, and medications.

Health AI can also assist with managing people's medications and book medical appointments for them with a One Medical provider. Health AI is available to people through the One Medical app. Amazon acquired One Medical for $3.9 billion in 2023. One Medical runs brick-and-mortar medical clinics and offers telehealth services to members who pay $99 to $199 a year for its services.

The Limits of Amazon's Health AI

Health AI has some limits. Amazon says it isn't intended to provide diagnosis or treatment for people, and it shouldn't replace a doctor's visit or trip to an Emergency Room in the event of a serious medical incident. The AI tool is mostly intended to identify symptoms and book in-person doctor visits for users.

Competitors of Amazon are also rolling out AI healthcare tools. Earlier in January, privately held OpenAI launched ChatGPT Health, a tool that enables people to upload medical records and receive personalized advice. Another AI startup, Anthropic, has released Claude for Healthcare, which helps people with healthcare issues.

Is AMZN Stock a Buy?

AMZN stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 47 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 46 Buy and one Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average AMZN price target of $294.93 implies 27.34% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Rally as Trump Withdraws Greenland Tariffs, Says Force Won't be Used in Acquisition Deal
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed higher on Wednesday after President Trump pulled back tariffs on eight European countries and announced a framework deal that wouldn't involve using military force to purchase Greenland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Alongside NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, Trump has formed a framework deal to acquire Greenland. Earlier in the day, he ruled out using force to take over the self-governing territory, easing geopolitical tensions.

"Based upon this understanding, I will not be imposing the Tariffs that were scheduled to go into effect on February 1st," Trump said in a Truth Social post. "Additional discussions are being held concerning The Golden Dome as it pertains to Greenland." Trump didn't specify the terms of the deal, although he told CNBC that it would be permanent with the details to be publicized soon. He added that the negotiations would be led by Vice President JD Vance, Secretary of State Marco Rubio, Special Envoy Steve Witkoff, and "various others."

Trump had previously stated that the tariffs would remain in effect until the U.S. reached a deal to purchase Greenland. Danish Foreign Minister Lars Løkke Rasmussen acknowledged the withdrawal of the tariffs as a positive sign, although he reiterated that Denmark wouldn't be able to accommodate Trump's requests.

Several financial leaders are in attendance at the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in Davos, Switzerland, including JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon and Citadel CEO and founder Ken Griffin. During a panel interview, Dimon warned that Trump's proposed credit card interest rate limit would be an "economic disaster" for Americans. "In the worst case, you'd have to have a drastic reduction of the credit card business," Dimon said. "You would remove credit from 80% of Americans and that is their backup credit."

Meanwhile, Griffin stressed that "recent research" indicates that the Trump administration's tariffs have "come at the expense of the American people." A study published this week by the Kiel Institute, a German research firm, concluded that Americans have paid for 96% of the tariffs, while foreign exporters have absorbed just 4%.

Looking ahead, the Bureau of Economic Analysis will release its second estimate of gross domestic product (GDP) for the third quarter on Thursday, while the Department of Labor will publish initial and continuing jobless claims.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 1.16% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 1.36%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Arista Networks (ANET) Traders Eye Breakout Following Stock Implosion
data.description:
Arista Networks (ANET) has struggled since early November, making exposure to ANET stock a higher-risk gamble. At the same time, it's fair to point out that the company offers a relevant business. As a developer and marketer of cloud networking solutions, Arista is likely to benefit from stronger tailwinds, such as the data center hardware refresh cycle. Subsequently, ANET's value compression may present an attractive opportunity for contrarians.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fundamentally, Arista's bullish case mostly centers on substantive metrics, particularly robust revenue growth. Indeed, the top line has accelerated due to rising demand in the artificial intelligence, cloud, and enterprise sectors, indicating an ideal market positioning. Further, the company enjoys financial flexibility thanks to its debt-free balance sheet and high return on equity. Finally, its acquisition of VeloCloud should bolster Arista's enterprise offerings, allowing it to expand its overall influence.
data.author: Joshua Enomoto

data.title: 'Meaningful Step Forward': Oklo Stock Jumps as BofA Goes Bullish on Meta Deal
data.description:
Oklo's (OKLO) shares climbed about 5% early Wednesday after Bank of America (BofA) (BAC) turned bullish on the stock, calling the nuclear startup's new deal with Meta (META) a "meaningful step forward."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Earlier this month, Oklo ignited hope in its advanced nuclear technology after it disclosed that it has secured a long-term 1.2 gigawatt supply contract from Meta. The tech supermajor also tapped two other companies for nuclear energy; they are Vistra Energy (VST) and billionaire Bill Gates-backed TerraPower.

BofA Upbeat after Oklo's 'Modest' Deal with Meta

Responding to the update, BofA analyst Dimple Gosai, who was previously bearish Oklo due to investor concern about the startup's capital expenditure and the timing of its power purchase agreements with clients — upgraded Oklo from Hold to Buy.

Gosai raised her OKLO price target by 14% from $111 per share to $127, implying more than 41% upside. The analyst noted that while the capacity to be supplied is "modest," the deal marks a "meaningful step forward" and offers concrete evidence of Oklo's execution and its partner's commitment.

Furthermore, the BofA analyst noted that Meta's decision to invest capital years before the project comes online highlights how critical nuclear power has become as AI and data center energy needs ramp up.

Oklo's Losses Widen

The analyst's upbeat comment comes even as Oklo in its recent third-quarter results released in November reported a worse-than-expected net loss. Gosai previously said Oklo has made "constructive" progress by securing regulatory approvals, thereby reducing the risks associated with its fuel source.

Oklo, which remains unprofitable, broke ground on its first Aurora nuclear reactor plant in late September. The startup has said it is working to deliver its first nuclear power plant by 2028.

Is Oklo a Good Stock to Buy?

Across Wall Street, analysts' consensus rating for Oklo remains a Moderate Buy. This is based on eight Buys and four Holds issued by 12 analysts over the past three months.

However, the average OKLO price target of $126.89 implies about 41% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo
